projects.title: After Hours
projects.tagline: A tense psychological horror prototype set in a mysterious, eerie office space.
projects.description:
After Hours is a downloadable horror prototype that plunges players into a claustrophobic and atmospheric environment. Originally created for the Brackeys Game Jam 2026.1, the project has evolved into a detailed study of tension and environmental storytelling.

You explore a strange, interactive office filled with secrets. Every object tells a story, and every shadow feels heavier than the last. The game features realistic computer interactions, a complex vent system, and a dynamic audio experience that keeps you on edge.

Key features include character-by-character text rendering, interactive props like ladders and buckets, and a progression system that leads to multiple unique discovery points and a complete ending.

Important reminder for players: You run with SHIFT!

mechanics.subtitle: Interactive Terminals
mechanics.description: Functional computer systems where text renders character-by-character, allowing for a more immersive and realistic hacking/exploration experience.

mechanics.subtitle: Environmental Navigation
mechanics.description: A claustrophobic vent system that requires players to use ladders and navigate tight spaces to uncover the deeper secrets of the facility.

mechanics.subtitle: Dynamic Atmosphere
mechanics.description: A combination of scheduled announcements, radio triggers, and visual effects like blood room transitions to create a constantly shifting sense of dread.
